record_identity.title: Disputed AI-enabled workforce substitution after negotiated safeguard adoption

summary: A healthcare labour dispute raises an unresolved governance question about whether AI-enabled workflow redesign displaced twelve utilization-review nurses after contractual AI safeguards had reportedly been negotiated. The union characterises the layoffs as replacement by AI and a contractual breach; Montefiore disputes that account and describes the technology changes as a nonclinical paperwork program.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: A safeguard can exist formally yet fail in implementation if organisations classify AI-mediated task substitution as workflow redesign, administrative automation, vendor support, or nonclinical activity outside the safeguard's trigger. The present evidence does not establish that bypass occurred, but it justifies review of functional-effect tests, workforce consultation, clinical-adjacency, data custody, and contractual conformance.

source_records.source_title: Incident 1593: Purportedly AI-Powered Software Allegedly Used to Replace Montefiore Hospital Utilization-Review Nurses Despite Contractual AI Safeguards
source_records.system_or_product: healthcare administrative AI and utilization-review software
source_records.model_or_algorithm: not established in the reviewed public material
source_records.deployment_context: Reported workflow change and layoffs affecting twelve utilization-review nurses at Montefiore Hospital.
source_records.source_context: AIID records three reports and expressly preserves the dispute: the union alleges AI replacement and contractual breach, while Montefiore describes nonclinical paperwork changes and disputes the characterisation.
source_records.relevance_note: Structured incident record establishing the contested proposition and evidence imbalance that justify observation rather than failure-mode classification.

primary_artefact_access.reviewing_system: OpenAI Codex in ChatGPT Work Mode
limitations: AIID is a secondary incident registry.
The actual collective-bargaining clause, software documentation, workflow map, and patient-data terms were not available.

source_records.interpretive_reliance: Used to preserve the disputed posture, timeline, and report set without resolving the underlying contractual or technical facts.

source_records.source_title: Bronx Nurses Sound Alarm on Montefiore Replacing Real Nurses with AI
source_records.deployment_context: Union media advisory concerning planned layoffs, utilization-review work, patient coverage review, and patient-record handling.
source_records.source_context: The union states that nurses use clinical judgement to review insurance denials and alleges that AI-powered software would replace them. It also raises patient-care, workforce, and private-record concerns.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary union-position source; establishes the workers' claim but not the employer's technical implementation or contractual breach.

primary_artefact_access.reviewing_system: OpenAI Codex in ChatGPT Work Mode
limitations: The source is an advocacy and labour-party statement.
It does not reproduce the governing contract, software specification, or employer workflow evidence.

source_records.interpretive_reliance: Relied upon only for the union's stated allegations, role description, and asserted harms.

source_records.source_title: The New York nurses replaced by AI: 'It should concern every patient who cares about quality of care'
source_records.system_or_product: Montefiore utilization-review workflow and unspecified AI-powered software
source_records.deployment_context: Interviews and reporting concerning twelve layoffs, changed workflows, negotiated AI safeguards, and Montefiore's response.
source_records.source_context: The report quotes affected nurses and union representatives asserting AI replacement and contractual breach. It also records Montefiore's response that the claims are inaccurate and misleading and that the technology changes concern a nonclinical paperwork program.
source_records.relevance_note: Provides the clearest reviewed source containing materially opposed accounts in one report.

primary_artefact_access.reviewing_system: OpenAI Codex in ChatGPT Work Mode
limitations: Montefiore did not comment specifically on the layoffs in the quoted response.
The underlying contractual and technical documents were not reproduced.
The article's illustrative photograph concerns a separate 2023 nursing strike.

source_records.interpretive_reliance: Used to preserve both parties' public positions and identify the unresolved evidence required before failure-mode classification.

system_context.interaction_mode: AI-enabled workflow redesign with disputed human task substitution
system_context.embodiment_status: non-embodied software with employment, healthcare, and data-governance effects
system_context.deployment_context: Hospital utilization review and administrative workflow following collective bargaining that reportedly included AI safeguards.
system_context.user_role: utilization-review nurse, clinician, administrator, insurer liaison, patient, union representative, vendor, or employer

secondary_jurisdictions: global relevance for AI-related workforce agreements and healthcare administration
jurisdictional_context.sector: healthcare / labour / insurance utilization review / administrative AI
jurisdictional_context.cross_border_relevance: high because functional AI-substitution and safeguard-trigger disputes can recur under different labour and healthcare regimes

migration_notes: This observation preserves a disputed governance proposition. It does not classify contractual breach, patient harm, safeguard bypass, or clinical substitution as established fact.
next_action: Obtain and review the actual contractual AI safeguard, before-and-after workflow map, software and vendor description, task-allocation evidence, patient-data handling terms, layoff rationale, and any grievance or adjudication outcome before deciding whether the event warrants a failure-mode or CAM proposal.

cam_internal.governance_layer: AI-enabled workforce substitution / negotiated safeguards / functional-effect classification / healthcare administrative workflow / data custody
cam_internal.routing_note: OBS routing only. The evidence is materially disputed and insufficient to classify safeguard bypass, patient harm, or contractual breach as a confirmed failure mode.
cam_internal.validator_or_automation_impact: new observation record; generated indexes and public catalogue should rebuild deterministically

possible_taxonomy_mapping.mapping_note: If later evidence confirms that AI-mediated task substitution was intentionally or functionally classified outside an applicable safeguard, the strongest mappings would be economic-legitimacy and governance, with classification and execution interfaces.
